$description: Print-only spacing scale for whitepapers, briefings, and other paged PDF formats. Values are in inches because print uses physical units, not the 4px web grid. Every gap has a floor AND a ceiling per the design-system rule that AI agents optimize toward maximums without bounds.

letter.$description: US Letter page size. Default for Verdigris briefings.

a4.$description: A4 page size. Use for international/EU audiences.

top.$description: Cover top margin. Generous to give the title block sky.

bottom.$description: Cover bottom margin. Floats the byline above the page edge.

margin-bottom.$description: Larger to leave room for running footer.

deck-to-byline-min.$description: Non-negotiable floor. Below this, the cover reads as a page-1 article.

deck-to-byline-max.$description: Non-negotiable ceiling. Above this, the cover reads as wrong-page-size.

padding-bottom.$description: Vertical rhythm between body sections inside the paper.

body.$description: Inside-pages spec for whitepapers. Sister to cover. Genre-conditional (lab_tradition uses dot-leader TOC + per-chapter numbering + chapter page breaks; policy_brief and ceo_brief simpler). All values floor + ceiling per project rule.

running-header-height-min.$description: Floor for the per-page running header band. Below 0.4in, the header crowds the body type and loses its function as orientation. Genre-conditional (lab + policy use; CEO brief often suppresses).

running-header-height-max.$description: Ceiling for the running header. Above 0.6in, the header eats into the body block and the page reads top-heavy.

running-footer-height-min.$description: Floor for the running footer band (page number, confidentiality marking). Below 0.4in, the footer touches the last line of body.

running-footer-height-max.$description: Ceiling for the running footer. Above 0.6in, the footer claims more page than it earns.

chapter-break-top-padding-min.$description: Floor for chapter title top padding. Lab convention drops the chapter title into the upper-third of the page; below 1.2in the title fails to read as a fresh chapter.

chapter-break-top-padding-max.$description: Ceiling for chapter title top padding. Above 2.0in the title floats too low and wastes a full page.

toc-entry-gap-min.$description: Floor for vertical gap between TOC entries. Below 6pt, entries crowd and lose scannability.

toc-entry-gap-max.$description: Ceiling for TOC entry gap. Above 12pt, the TOC sprawls over multiple pages without earning the space.

toc-indent-per-level.$description: Indent applied per heading level (h2 -- 0; h3 -- 1x indent; h4 -- 2x indent). Single fixed value; hierarchy reads at 0.25in steps.

figure-gap-above-min.$description: Floor for the gap above a body figure. Below 18pt the figure fuses with the preceding paragraph.

figure-gap-above-max.$description: Ceiling for the gap above a body figure. Above 28pt the figure floats away from the prose that introduces it.

figure-gap-below-min.$description: Floor for the gap below a figure caption. Smaller than the gap above because the caption already separates the figure from following prose.

figure-gap-below-max.$description: Ceiling for the gap below a figure caption. Above 22pt the next paragraph reads as a fresh section, not a continuation.

pullquote-side-margin-min.$description: Floor for pull-quote side indent from the text column. Below 0.5in the pull-quote does not visually detach from body prose.

pullquote-side-margin-max.$description: Ceiling for pull-quote side indent. Above 1.0in the pull-quote becomes a thin column and loses oversized-type weight.

body-text-line-height.$description: Body paragraph leading. 1.55 matches cover.css body and reads comfortably at 10.5pt over a 7in measure.

body-paragraph-spacing.$description: Paragraph-to-paragraph vertical spacing in body prose. Smaller than section spacing so paragraphs read as continuous argument.

pullquote-vertical-spacing-min.$description: Floor for the gap above and below a pull-quote in surrounding flow. Below 18pt the pull-quote does not read as a discrete block.

pullquote-vertical-spacing-max.$description: Ceiling for pull-quote vertical spacing. Above 32pt the pull-quote isolates itself from the argument that earned it.

table-cell-padding-y-min.$description: Floor for table cell vertical padding. Below 4pt, rows crush; tabular comparison fails.

table-cell-padding-y-max.$description: Ceiling for table cell vertical padding. Above 8pt, the table reads as cards, not a comparison grid.

table-cell-padding-x-min.$description: Floor for table cell horizontal padding. Below 8pt, columns abut and become hard to scan.

table-cell-padding-x-max.$description: Ceiling for table cell horizontal padding. Above 14pt, narrow columns wrap unnecessarily.

equation-vertical-spacing-min.$description: Floor for vertical air above and below a display equation. Below 14pt the equation collides with surrounding prose.

equation-vertical-spacing-max.$description: Ceiling for equation vertical air. Above 24pt the equation feels like a figure rather than an inline mathematical step.

references-entry-gap-min.$description: Floor for vertical gap between bibliography entries. Below 6pt, entries fuse and the reference list fails to scan.

references-entry-gap-max.$description: Ceiling for reference entry gap. Above 10pt, the bibliography over-claims pages without improving readability.
